title: The Crew: The Quest for Planet Nine

subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (from provided rulebook text)
- **Core player range** — 3-5 players for standard game.
- **2-player mode** — Separate variant section provided (Playing with Two Players).
- **5-player mode** — Additional mission rule from mission 25 onward (gold frame missions).
- **3-player dealing exception** — One player gets one extra card; one card remains unplayed after final trick.
- **3-player challenge variant** — Optional variant removes all green cards/task cards and Rocket 1.

#### Cards
- **Large playing cards** — 40 total.
- **Color cards** — 36 total (4 colors, values 1-9).
- **Rocket cards** — 4 total (values 1-4).
- **Task cards** — 36 small cards.
- **Reminder cards** — 5 large reminder cards.

#### Tokens and Markers
- **Total tokens** — 16.
- **Task tokens** — 10.
- **Radio communication tokens** — 5.
- **Distress signal token** — 1.
- **Commander token** — 1.

#### Reference Material
- **Rulebook/logbook** — 1 (includes 50 missions).

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Standard Mission Preparation (3-5 players)
1. **Deal deck** — Shuffle 40 large cards and deal face down as evenly as possible.
2. **Radio tokens** — Each player places one radio token green side up.
3. **Reminder cards** — Keep one reminder card ready per player.
4. **Distress signal** — Place the distress signal token face down.
5. **Task deck** — Shuffle 36 task cards and place face down.

#### Player-Count Dealing Differences
--- 3 Players ---
- **Uneven deal rule** — One player receives one extra card.
- **Unplayed card rule** — After the last trick, one card remains unplayed.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Deal pattern** — Deal 40 cards evenly (10 each).

--- 5 Players ---
- **Deal pattern** — Deal 40 cards evenly (8 each).

--- 2 Players (Variant) ---
- **Variant note** — Use the dedicated two-player structure from the rulebook section "Playing with Two Players.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Start of Mission
1. **Reveal tasks** — Reveal the mission's required number of task cards in center.
2. **Find commander** — Player with Rocket 4 takes commander token.
3. **Draft tasks** — Commander chooses first task, then draft continues clockwise one task at a time.
4. **Begin communication window** — Communication is allowed only after task distribution is complete.
5. **Lead first trick** — Commander leads unless mission text says otherwise.

#### Player-Count Notes
--- 3-5 Players ---
- **Commander identity** — Rocket 4 holder is commander.
- **Task spread** — Players may end with uneven task counts depending on mission and draft.

--- 2 Players (Variant) ---
- **Role handling** — Rocket 4 holder is commander and also manages JARVIS as a simulated third crew member.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Mission Flow (sequence)
1. **Mission preparation** — Deal cards and prepare tokens/task deck.
2. **Task distribution** — Reveal and assign tasks according to mission rules.
3. **Trick loop**
   - **Lead card** — Leader sets the suit.
   - **Clockwise play** — Each player plays one card.
   - **Follow suit** — Must follow led suit if possible.
   - **Resolve winner** — Highest card of led suit wins, unless rockets are present (highest rocket wins).
   - **Next leader** — Trick winner leads next trick.
4. **Mission check**
   - **Success** — All assigned tasks completed by correct owners.
   - **Failure** — Any wrong-player capture of a required card or mission-rule violation.
5. **After outcome**
   - **On success** — Proceed to next mission.
   - **On failure** — Reshuffle and replay mission attempt.

#### Player-Count Structure Notes
--- 3-5 Players ---
- **Base flow** — Standard sequence above.

--- 2 Players (Variant) ---
- **Flow adaptation** — Commander also chooses and plays for JARVIS according to two-player rules.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Trick Play Options
- **Card play obligation** — Play exactly one card on your turn in each trick.
- **Follow-suit rule** — If you have the led suit, you must play that suit.
- **Off-suit freedom** — If you cannot follow suit, you may play any card.
- **No forced overtake** — You are not required to play a winning card.

#### Rocket Rules
- **Trump status** — Rockets beat all color suits.
- **Rocket-on-rocket** — Highest rocket wins among rockets played.
- **When rocket is led** — Rockets become the suit to follow, if possible.
- **Communication restriction** — Rocket cards can never be communicated.

#### Communication Action (once per player per mission attempt)
- **Timing** — Only before a trick, never during a trick.
- **Card shown** — Place one color card face up (not a rocket).
- **Token positions**
  - **Top** — This is your highest card of that color.
  - **Middle** — This is your only card of that color.
  - **Bottom** — This is your lowest card of that color.
- **Validity rule** — One of those statements must be true at the time you communicate.
- **No token movement** — Token position cannot be changed later.
- **Use tracking** — Flip token red after communication card is played.

#### Task Completion Actions
- **Task fulfill** — Win a trick containing the matching playing card for your task card.
- **Multiple fulfills** — One trick can complete multiple tasks for the same player.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Mission-Based Win Structure
- **Campaign model** — 50 missions with escalating constraints.
- **Task ownership** — Each task card belongs to one player and must be fulfilled by that player.
- **Immediate fail trigger** — Wrong player winning a required card can end the attempt instantly.

#### Hidden Information Discipline
- **No hand talk** — You cannot show, say, or suggest your hand cards outside permitted communication.
- **Public trick history limit** — Won tricks are face down; only the most recently won trick may be reviewed.

#### Player-Count Variants
--- 5 Players ---
- **Additional rule timing** — From mission 25 onward (gold-framed mission numbers).
- **Task handover step** — After cards/tasks are distributed and before first trick, one player may hand one of their task cards to another player.
- **Information restriction** — Decide handover without revealing card information.
- **Task-count constraint** — After handover, no player may have two more tasks than another (for commander's distribution context).

--- 2 Players (Variant) ---
- **JARVIS setup** — 14-card double row (7 face down + 7 face up on top).
- **Commander role expansion** — Commander chooses tasks for JARVIS and chooses which face-up JARVIS card is played.
- **Reveal timing** — When a JARVIS face-up card is played, the card below is revealed only after the trick ends.

--- 3 Players (Challenge Variant) ---
- **Removed cards** — Return all green playing cards and green task cards to box.
- **Removed rocket** — Return Rocket 1 to box.
- **Mission impact note** — Missions 13 and 44 are adjusted by that removal according to rulebook note.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Mission Resolution
- **No VP system** — The game uses pass/fail mission outcomes, not points.
- **Mission success** — All task cards are completed by their owners and all mission-specific rules are respected.
- **Mission failure** — A required card is won by non-owner, or a mission rule is violated.

#### After Success
- **Progression** — Move to the next mission.
- **Logbook use** — Record number of attempts if desired by campaign tracking.

#### After Failure
- **Reset** — Reshuffle cards, redeal, redistribute tasks, and retry mission.

#### Tie/Comparison Handling
- **No tie-breakers** — No comparative scoring because play is cooperative pass/fail.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### First Tricks Priorities
- **Map your task targets** — Identify which suit/values are most likely to route to you.
- **Commander first pick discipline** — Choose tasks aligned with cards you can likely control.
- **Preserve rockets** — Keep rockets for must-win task moments, not casual tempo wins.
- **Lead control awareness** — Decide early which teammate should control next lead.

#### Communication Priorities
- **High-impact signal only** — Spend your one communication on information that changes decisions immediately.
- **Sequence before signal** — Complete task distribution first, then communicate legally before a trick.

#### Player-Count Emphasis
--- 3 Players ---
- **Card-load caution** — One player has an extra card and one unplayed card exists; check task routing assumptions carefully.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Compression pressure** — Fewer cards per player means tighter timing on task control; avoid wasting early lead opportunities.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### High-Frequency Errors
- **Early low-value communication** — Burns your one signal before critical mission moments.
- **Rocket overuse** — Spending trumps on non-critical tricks leaves no rescue options later.
- **Ignoring wrong-owner risk** — Winning a trick can be bad if it captures another player's required card.
- **Lead mismanagement** — Taking an unnecessary trick can force a dangerous next lead.
- **Illegal hinting** — Sharing hand info outside the communication system breaks core rules.

#### Player-Count Error Traps
--- 3 Players ---
- **Forgotten unplayed card** — Miscounting card availability causes bad probability reads.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Task handover misuse (mission 25+)** — Skipping the allowed handover optimization in golden-frame missions can increase failure rate.

--- 2 Players (Variant) ---
- **JARVIS reveal timing mistake** — Revealing the lower card before trick completion is incorrect.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Control vs Concealment
- **Control line** — Play high to lock a critical task now.
- **Concealment line** — Play low to preserve hand structure and keep options hidden.

#### Decision 2: Communication Timing
- **Early signal** — Clarifies opening routes but may be wasted if mission state changes.
- **Late signal** — Saves precision for endgame but risks early miscoordination.

#### Decision 3: Lead Ownership
- **Take lead now** — Choose next suit directly when your hand can drive task completion.
- **Pass lead intentionally** — Lose current trick to give suit choice to a better-positioned teammate.

#### Player-Count Context
--- 5 Players (mission 25+ with golden frame) ---
- **Task handover choice** — Decide whether shifting one task creates a more reliable ownership map before trick 1.

--- 2 Players (Variant) ---
- **Commander burden choice** — Decide when to optimize JARVIS for task security versus preserving your own hand flexibility.

metadata.notes: Written from provided The Crew rulebook excerpt (materials, trick rules, communication, mission prep/sequence).
